title: Conjecture 1 (Tang, Liu, and Wang)
statement_text: For every connected graph $G$ of order $n$ and $p\geq 2$, $$\mathcal{E}^{+}_{p}(G)\geq\mathcal{E}^{+}_{p}(P_{n}).$$
context_text: Tang, Liu, and Wang initiated the study of positive and negative $p$-energies of graphs and posed this conjecture about the minimality of the path $P_n$ for positive $p$-energy among connected graphs. The present paper poses and partially settles an analogous conjecture for negative $p$-energy.
paper_contribution: The paper does not directly settle Conjecture 1; instead it poses and proves the negative $p$-energy analogue (Conjecture 2) for $p\geq 4$, and establishes sub-matrix energy inequalities giving lower bounds for $p$-energy via vertex partitioning.

title: Conjecture 2
statement_text: For every connected graph $G$ of order $n$ and $p\geq 2$, $$\mathcal{E}^{-}_{p}(G)\geq\mathcal{E}^{-}_{p}(P_{n}).$$
context_text: The paper's authors pose this as the natural negative $p$-energy analogue of Conjecture 1 of Tang, Liu, and Wang. Together, Conjectures 1 and 2 generalize Conjecture 3 of Elphick, Farber, Goldberg, and Wocjan, since at $p=2$ both give the bound $n-1$ by symmetry of the path spectrum. The paper settles this conjecture for $p\geq 4$ via Theorem 1.
notes: The theorem-environment text captures only the preamble clause; the displayed formula $\mathcal{E}^{-}_{p}(G)\geq\mathcal{E}^{-}_{p}(P_{n})$ is reconstructed from the stated analogy with Conjecture 1, the generalisation claim for Conjecture 3, and the fact that Theorem 1 (proved bound $\mathcal{E}_{p}^{-}(G)\geq n$ for $p\geq 4$, $G\ncong K_n$) is said to settle this conjecture for $p\geq 4$. The case $2\leq p<4$ remains open.

title: Conjecture 3 (Elphick, Farber, Goldberg, and Wocjan)
statement_text: For every connected graph $G$ of order $n$, $$\min\{\mathcal{E}_{2}^{+}(G),\mathcal{E}_{2}^{-}(G)\}\geq n-1.$$
context_text: Posed by Elphick, Farber, Goldberg, and Wocjan in 2016, this conjecture is a special case ($p=2$) of Conjectures 1 and 2. Zhang proved the linear bound $\min\{\mathcal{E}_{2}^{+}(G),\mathcal{E}_{2}^{-}(G)\}\geq n/2$ via the domination number, and the same authors as the present paper proved $3n/4$ for $n\geq 4$ in a companion work, but the conjecture remains open.
paper_contribution: The paper develops sub-matrix block energy inequalities and vertex-partitioning lower bounds for $p$-energy that are relevant tools for this conjecture, but explicitly states that Conjecture 3 still remains elusive.
